title: Who becomes an Atheist? | Bhagavad Gita Verses 9.12 - 9.13 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

mam evaye prapadyante mayam etam tarantite, if you surrender to Me,

If you become a law-abiding citizen, if you reform yourself, just like we have in a normal prison house, you go into a prison house because you have violated the state laws, you have indulged in some criminal activity. When you get reformed, then you are eligible to come out of the prison house.
Therefore Krishna says, maam eva ye prapadyante, if you surrender to me, mayam etam taranti te, I will free you from maya.
Now there are two types of maya. One is called mahamaya. Mahamaya is what we experience in this world and one is yogamaya. Yogamaya is Daiviprakriti, Lord's internal potency.
So right now we are under the influence of mahamaya. The Mahatmas, great souls, are under the influence of Daiviprakriti. They are not under the influence of mahamaya.
Now one of the requirements, why we are entangled in this material world, can anyone tell me why we are, kyun maya jaal mein phasay hoon hai?
What has really bonded, so to say entangled us in this material world?
Hum log kyun maya jaal mein phas gaye hain? Why? What was the beginning point? What was the starting point?
Because we have been through a lot of experience.
So God let us free into a world of creation.
The answer is Ichcha. Ichcha means desire and Dvesha. Dvesha means envy.
Ichcha, desire to enjoy independent of the Lord and Dvesha means envious of Lord.
When one develops Ichcha, desire to enjoy independently. How do we desire?
Instead of serving the Lord, when we desire that yes I want to be the Lord, then instead of becoming so called Lords, we become servants of our senses.
